topic: North Magnetic Pole deviation >18° from 120°E longitude
our_argument_summary: Verdict 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold. c5 verifies the EXP-291 single-sentence insertion landed correctly in WIN-036 detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-036 — sentence 'Both NOAA's WMM2025 secular-variation forecast and the dome's purported exponential trajectory are fits to the same public NOAA NP.xy time series; they have not been shown to make distinguishable predictions at any future epoch.' present; detail_evidence length 1154 chars matches EXP-291 replacement_word_count.detail_evidence_chars_new). The insertion materially weakens the c3 advocate steel-man 'our exponential trajectory was committed before WMM2025 was published' by demanding a discrimination test rather than asserting parallel-readout equivalence outright. defense_survives drops 3 → 2 (not 1) — see hole #2 below for the residual rebuttal vector.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c4. PROS-006 registers a static threshold (>18° east of 120°E meridian); the dome's confirmation transcribes WMM2025's published pole position (139.298°E) and computes 139.298 − 120 = 19.298° exceeding 18° by 1.298°. No live monitoring domain in monitor.py is dedicated to the >18°/120°E test.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Unchanged from c4. Hardcoded transcription (raw-text/02-wins.txt:124, 03-predictions.txt:41). No API call computes this against a live WMM2025 endpoint.
data_sources_used: NOAA NP.xy historical pole-position record (manual transcription, no live fetch)
WMM2025 (referenced in raw-text but not fetched — see hole #2 for a precision concern about how WMM2025 is built vs. how the dome trajectory is built)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — the threshold is a binary >18° gate.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new in c5. The c4 tag set (monitoring=hardcoded, relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false) remains correct and unchanged by the EXP-291 insertion.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Tag unchanged from c4. WIN-036 (W010) confirmation is a static line in raw-text/02-wins.txt:124 and raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41,67. No monitor.py domain dedicated to the >18°/120°E test. EXP-291 inserts one sentence into detail_evidence; it does not touch monitor.py automation. Tag holds.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Tag unchanged from c4. WMM2025 (NOAA/BGS, Dec 2024) publishes the NMP at ~139.298°E; the 19.298° figure relative to 120°E is the same pole position re-expressed in a different coordinate frame. Standard geophysics (Livermore et al. 2020, Nat. Geosci., DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9, cited via WIN-007) explains the post-1990 acceleration via flux-lobe dynamics. The dome relabels the cause as 'aetheric-vortex threshold crossing' but predicts no different value than WMM2025. Tag holds.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Tag unchanged from c4. WMM2025 published 139.298°E in Dec 2024 (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41); PROS-006 registered 2026-03-06 — over 14 months later. The 18° threshold was already exceeded by 1.298° at registration time. EXP-291 does not change registration-time facts; it strengthens the post-hoc characterization by adding the 'no demonstrated divergent prediction' framing.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Tag unchanged from c4. 120°E is a geographic reference meridian; 18° is a chosen threshold. Neither is derived from disc_radius=20,015 km or firmament_height=9,086 km. The EXP-291 inserted sentence explicitly characterizes the dome's exponential trajectory as a 'fit' (data-driven curve, not a derivation) — consistent with the derives_from_dome=false tag.

holes_found.description: Cross-page OTS-canary tension PERSISTS post-ISS-1867 closure. ISS-1867 (closed 2026-05-07T01:16:52Z, fixed_by decider-self-apply) was scoped narrowly: it harmonized the language ACROSS WINs 031/035/036/037/038/039 prose to read 'OTS timestamps status_history.json, which per-domain bundles prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance threshold, source label) together with observation-side fields (observed value, error percentage, pass/fail)' (verified NEW-LANG present in WIN-035/036/037/038/039 detail_verdict_text). However, the Timestamp Error tab in build-scripts/generate-html.js:1832 still reads 'OpenTimestamps anchors status_history.json, the file containing reference data: observed values, pass/fail results, and statistical comparisons. These are observations — the outcomes. The prediction parameters — the formulas, expected values, and tolerances — live in monitor.py source code and docs/model.html, which are only git-versioned, not blockchain-timestamped.' These two characterizations are mutually inconsistent: the WIN prose explicitly lists 'formula' as one of the fields bundled into status_history.json, while the Timestamp Error tab claims formulas live ONLY in monitor.py. A defender can rebut: 'Pick one — does status_history.json contain prediction-side parameters (your WIN-036 says it does) or only observations (your Timestamp Error tab says it does)? You can't both.' The c3 review explicitly demanded site-wide harmonization; the executed fix was one-sided. Tracked: this is the second cycle in a row this hole has been flagged as MAJOR (c3, c4, now c5). ISS-1867 was prematurely closed before the cross-page harmonization the issue's own description called for ('update language in all 6 OTS WINs consistently' was achieved; the implicit cross-page consistency with the Timestamp Error tab was not).
holes_found.recommendation: Reopen the issue (or open a new ISS) and pick one consistent characterization site-wide. Two coherent options: (A) Update the Timestamp Error tab to acknowledge that status_history.json contains both prediction-side descriptor fields (formula label, predicted value, tolerance threshold, source label) AND observation-side result fields, then explain why the prediction-side descriptors are not equivalent to the prediction LOGIC (the executable code that produces them lives in monitor.py and can be re-edited at any commit). (B) Revert WINs 031/035/036/037/038/039 prose back to a higher-level framing that says 'OTS timestamps the observations file; the prediction logic lives in mutable source code' without the field-by-field specificity that conflicts with the Timestamp Error tab. Option A is more honest (status_history.json really does contain the prediction-side label fields per the new wording); option B is shorter but loses the precision of the new framing. Either way, the build-scripts/generate-html.js Timestamp Error tab must move. Until reconciled, this is a defender-discoverable contradiction across two prominent surfaces of our review.

holes_found.description: EXP-291 inserted sentence asserts a technically imprecise common-source claim. The sentence reads: 'Both NOAA's WMM2025 secular-variation forecast and the dome's purported exponential trajectory are fits to the same public NOAA NP.xy time series; they have not been shown to make distinguishable predictions at any future epoch.' The phrase 'fits to the same public NOAA NP.xy time series' is technically inaccurate for WMM2025. WMM2025 is built from Swarm satellite vector magnetic field measurements, ground observatory data, and prior IGRF coefficients via a Gauss spherical harmonic inversion (the IGRF-13/14 process); the North Magnetic Pole position (NP.xy) is a DERIVED OUTPUT of the model — the location of the dipole axis (or equivalent) computed from the inverted field. The dome's exponential trajectory IS plausibly a fit to historical NP.xy positions. The sentence collapses these into 'both are fits to NP.xy', which conflates input with output. EXP-291's anticipated_objections #3 explicitly acknowledged this issue and offered the alternative wording 'fits to the same public NOAA NMP trajectory record' as 'acceptable wording' if WIN-036 prose 'needs the precision', but the analyst chose not to adopt the more precise version. A geomagnetism-literate dome defender can rebut: 'WMM2025 ingests Swarm vector measurements through Gauss-coefficient inversion. NP.xy is what WMM2025 PRODUCES. We (the dome) curve-fit NP.xy. We are not doing the same thing as NOAA. Your sentence claims we are.' This residual rebuttal vector is why defense_survives drops 3 → 2 rather than 3 → 1.
holes_found.recommendation: One-sentence wording fix in WIN-036 detail_evidence. Replace 'are fits to the same public NOAA NP.xy time series' with one of: (A) 'are constrained by the same NOAA-authored North Magnetic Pole trajectory record' (preserves common-source claim, avoids input/output conflation); (B) 'depend on the same publicly archived geomagnetic measurement program (Swarm vector measurements, ground observatories, and the historical NMP trajectory)' (more verbose but technically airtight). Option A is the EXP-291 author's own preferred fallback per anticipated_objections #3 response. Either fix takes the same number of characters give or take, requires no other field updates.

holes_found.description: EXP-291 inserted sentence uses 'secular-variation forecast' as a technical term without definition. A non-specialist reader following along may not know that 'secular variation' in geomagnetism means the slow time variation of the geomagnetic field (timescales of decades to millennia). The TLDRs (tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) are plain-language so this jargon is not exposed in the summary surface, but the detail_evidence prose now contains it without a parenthetical gloss. Cosmetic; the sentence is comprehensible from context, and 'forecast' carries most of the semantic load. Flag for analyst awareness only.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: replace 'WMM2025 secular-variation forecast' with 'WMM2025 model (which forecasts the geomagnetic field's slow time variation, including pole drift)' on a future cleanup pass. Not blocking.

stronger_arguments.description: Be specific about the build pipelines. The strongest version of the common-source argument names the actual data flow: 'WMM2025 ingests Swarm satellite vector measurements (2014-2024), ground observatory data, and prior IGRF coefficients through a Gauss spherical harmonic inversion; the dome's exponential trajectory is fit to the published NMP positions (NP.xy) that WMM and predecessor models produce as a derived output. Both pipelines depend on the same publicly archived geomagnetic measurement program. Whether the dome's exponential and WMM2025's secular-variation polynomial would diverge at any future epoch has not been demonstrated.' This version is harder to attack on technical grounds (no input/output conflation) and makes the divergence-test invitation explicit.

Name a concrete discrimination test. WMM2025's 5-year epoch publishes year-by-year pole positions through 2030. A genuinely independent dome prediction would commit (via a properly-anchored OTS scheme that timestamps a predictions-only document — see Timestamp Error tab) a divergent pole position for, e.g., epoch 2030.0 (i.e., 2030-01-01) before the WMM2030 epoch update is published in late 2029. The dome has 1.5 years of runway to do this. Naming this test concretely converts the inserted sentence's 'they have not been shown to make distinguishable predictions at any future epoch' from a passive observation into an actionable scientific challenge that the dome can either accept or visibly decline. Adding such a sentence to detail_extra (or as a final closing sentence in detail_evidence) would convert the WIN from 'we critique their post-hoc claim' to 'we critique their post-hoc claim AND offer them a path to a falsifiable prospective test' — a more credible adversarial posture.
sources: Timestamp Error tab (the proper-anchoring scheme the dome would need to adopt)

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-291's inserted sentence is a real and substantive improvement to WIN-036 (data/wins.json:WIN-036.detail_evidence). The c3/c4 advocate steel-man — 'our exponential trajectory was committed before WMM2025, so the threshold parameter is structurally bound to a dome trajectory model, not a copy of WMM2025's number' — exploited a real gap in the prior prose: nothing in the WIN explicitly demanded a divergent prospective prediction as the test of independence. The new sentence closes that gap by naming the discrimination criterion (distinguishable predictions at any future epoch) and observing it has not been demonstrated. This is a textbook curmudgeon-cycle defense neutralization: a single sentence that converts a steel-man defense into an own-goal by demanding the dome produce evidence it does not have (per data/wins.json:WIN-036.detail_evidence post-EXP-291). The kernel is genuine: the dome author DID build a trajectory model and registered a quantitative threshold; that's structurally the right shape for a scientific prediction. The author also did not produce a divergent prospective forecast; that absence is what the inserted sentence makes load-bearing.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The verdict 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold. The EXP-291 sentence does not change WHY the verdict holds — it strengthens HOW we explain that the dome confirmation is non-discriminating with respect to WMM2025 (per data/wins.json:WIN-036.detail_verdict_text and raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41). The remaining defense_survives=2 reflects two residual rebuttal vectors: (i) the 'fits to NP.xy' wording is technically imprecise about WMM2025's build pipeline (hole #2), and (ii) the c3 cross-page OTS-canary tension was only one-sidedly addressed by ISS-1867's closure (hole #1). Neither residual touches the verdict; both touch the bulletproofing of the prose around the verdict. The c5 patch round (EXP-291) is a strict improvement over c4; the residual holes are pre-existing and one is newly-introduced by the imprecise wording in the inserted sentence itself.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Two residual lines after EXP-291: (1) The technical pushback on 'fits to NP.xy' — 'WMM2025 isn't built from NP.xy. NP.xy is what WMM2025 produces. You're conflating input with output. We curve-fit NP.xy; NOAA does Gauss-coefficient inversion of Swarm satellite measurements. We are NOT doing the same thing.' This is a legitimate technical objection that survives the inserted sentence as written. (2) The OTS-canary cross-page contradiction — 'Your WIN-036 prose says status_history.json bundles prediction-side fields including FORMULA. Your Timestamp Error tab says formulas live in monitor.py, NOT status_history.json. Pick one. Until you do, the cross-page contradiction is a hole in your review, not in the dome's methodology.' Neither defense saves the dome from the post-hoc + non-discriminating verdict, but each provides a defender with a way to redirect attention from the substantive critique to a wording flaw in our review. defense_survives = 2.
advocate_mode.what_we_should_say_instead: Two-step fix to drop defense_survives to 1 or 0: (a) Replace 'fits to the same public NOAA NP.xy time series' with the EXP-291 author's own fallback wording 'constrained by the same NOAA-authored North Magnetic Pole trajectory record' (anticipated_objections #3 response, EXP-291.json). This neutralizes the technical pushback. (b) Either update the Timestamp Error tab to acknowledge prediction-side descriptor fields in status_history.json (with explanation that descriptors ≠ logic) or revert WIN-036/035/037/038/039 prose to higher-level framing without the field-list. Both fixes are scoped, mechanical, and well-understood — they were called out in c3, c4, and now c5. The work is queued; cycle 6 should be the closing pass.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: ONE persisting cross-page (not cross-WIN) inconsistency: WIN-035/036/037/038/039 prose now uniformly reads 'status_history.json...per-domain bundles prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance threshold, source label) together with observation-side fields (observed value, error percentage, pass/fail)' (NEW-LANG, verified across all five WINs). The Timestamp Error tab in build-scripts/generate-html.js:1832 still reads 'OpenTimestamps anchors status_history.json, the file containing reference data: observed values, pass/fail results, and statistical comparisons. These are observations — the outcomes. The prediction parameters — the formulas, expected values, and tolerances — live in monitor.py source code and docs/model.html.' The two surfaces contradict on whether status_history.json contains prediction-side fields (the WINs say yes; the Timestamp Error tab says no). WIN-031 still uses OTHER OTS language (different formulation, did not get the ISS-1867 sweep — possibly intentional because WIN-031 has different evidence structure, but worth a glance). WIN-007 not affected by OTS framing. Cross-WIN coherence within the 035-039 batch is fine; cross-PAGE coherence with the Timestamp Error tab is the open issue.
cross_win_consistency.actions_recommended: Pick one site-wide characterization (Option A: update Timestamp Error tab to admit prediction-side descriptors in status_history.json; Option B: revert WIN prose to high-level framing). Either is mechanical; neither requires analyst expansion. Decider can self-apply. WIN-031 OTS wording should be checked separately — out of scope for this review but worth flagging.

EXP-291 patch landed cleanly. Cross-page contradiction with Timestamp Error tab confirmed unresolved. No new arithmetic discrepancies.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: ISS-1867 closure verification pattern only checks NEW-LANG presence in data/wins.json — it does NOT check that build-scripts/generate-html.js was updated to match. The verifier passed because the WIN prose change landed; the cross-page consistency the original issue description called for was not enforced by the verifier. Process gap: ISS-1867's verification_pattern was scoped to one of the two files the harmonization needed to touch.

tldr_review.tldr_notes: TLDRs not modified by EXP-291 (per integration_notes 'No changes to TLDRs'). tldr_evidence (237 chars: 'WMM2025 (published December 2024) already placed the North Magnetic Pole at ~139.3°E — over 14 months before the dome registered its prediction in March 2026. The dome extrapolates the same NOAA trajectory data as the standard model.') and tldr_verdict (132 chars: 'The World Magnetic Model published this pole position over a year before the dome's prediction. Same data, no independent mechanism.') both remain factually correct after the c5 patch round. The TLDRs use the more general 'NOAA trajectory data' / 'same data' framing that avoids the input/output conflation issue raised in hole #2 — TLDRs are technically more careful than the new detail_evidence sentence, ironically.

deeper_analysis:
c5 was triggered by priority-queue item 308 (pushed_at 2026-05-07T09:18:06.348Z) verifying EXP-291's single-sentence insertion into WIN-036 detail_evidence. The insertion landed cleanly: detail_evidence length grew 925 → 1154 chars, exactly matching EXP-291.replacement_word_count.delta_chars=229; the inserted sentence is present byte-for-byte; no other WIN-036 fields were touched. The verdict 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold (confidence 0.92, slightly down from c4's 0.94 reflecting the new moderate hole #2 introduced by the imprecise 'fits to NP.xy' wording).

Defense_survives drops 3 → 2 (not the 3→1-or-2 hoped for in the queue reason). The inserted sentence MATERIALLY weakens the c3 advocate steel-man by demanding a discrimination test rather than letting the 'we predicted before WMM2025' line stand unrebutted — that is a real and substantive improvement. But two residuals remain:

(1) MAJOR: cross-page OTS-canary tension persists from c3/c4. ISS-1867 was closed when only the WIN prose was harmonized (NEW-LANG now consistent across WIN-035/036/037/038/039); the Timestamp Error tab in build-scripts/generate-html.js:1832 still uses the prior 'observations only' framing. The WIN prose now lists 'formula' as a status_history.json field; the Timestamp Error tab says formulas live in monitor.py only. ISS-1867's verification_pattern was scoped to data/wins.json grep — it did not enforce cross-page consistency. This is the third consecutive cycle (c3, c4, c5) flagging this hole; recommend reopening as a new ISS scoped to either updating the Timestamp Error tab or reverting the WIN prose to the high-level framing.

(2) MODERATE: the inserted sentence's 'fits to the same public NOAA NP.xy time series' framing is technically imprecise. WMM2025 is built from Swarm satellite vector measurements via Gauss-coefficient inversion (IGRF process); NP.xy is the OUTPUT of WMM2025, not the INPUT. The dome's exponential trajectory IS plausibly fit to NP.xy. EXP-291.anticipated_objections #3 acknowledged this and provided 'constrained by the same NOAA-authored North Magnetic Pole trajectory record' as an acceptable alternative; the analyst did not adopt it in the actual prose. A geomagnetism-literate defender can rebut on input/output grounds. One-sentence wording fix neutralizes this.

Both residuals are scoped, mechanical, and well-understood. The verdict is sound; the prose around the verdict needs one more sweep. Confidence 0.92 on the verdict; confidence on the prose-bulletproofing is lower (call it 0.85) because two cycles of decider self-apply have not yet closed both surfaces of the c3 finding. Cycle 6 should be the closing pass.

Minor honesty note: the queue reason said 'Expect defense_survives to drop 3→1 or 2'. I land at 2, which is the upper end of that prediction. The drop to 1 would have required EXP-291 to also fix the wording precision OR the Timestamp Error tab harmonization OR both. EXP-291 was scoped to one sentence in WIN-036 detail_evidence and the analyst hewed to that scope; the residuals are on other surfaces (Timestamp Error tab, sentence-precision) that EXP-291 explicitly excluded as out-of-scope.
